
Consider pseudocode:
```
COMPUTE-Z-ARRAY(S)
  n = length[S]
  Z[1] = 0
  l = r = 0
  for i = 2 to n
    if i > r
      l = r = i
      while r <= n and S[r-l+1] == S[r]
        r = r + 1
      Z[i] = r - l
      r = r - 1
    else
      k = i - l + 1
      if Z[k] < r - i + 1
        Z[i] = Z[k]
      else
        l = i
        while r <= n and S[r-l+1] == S[r]
          r = r + 1
        Z[i] = r - l
        r = r - 1
```

Pseudocode (naive STRSTR):
```
STRSTR(T, P)
  n = length[T]
  m = length[P]
  for i = 1 to n - m + 1
    match = TRUE
    for j = 1 to m
      if T[i+j-1] != P[j]
        match = FALSE
        break
    if match then return i
  return -1
```

Pseudocode (naive):
```
LAST-OCCURRENCE(T, P)
  n = length[T]
  m = length[P]
  for i = n - m + 1 downto 1
    match = TRUE
    for j = 1 to m
      if T[i+j-1] != P[j]
        match = FALSE
        break
    if match then return i
  return -1
```

Pseudocode:
```
LONGEST-COMMON-PREFIX(ARR)
  prefix = ARR[1]
  for k = 2 to length[ARR]
    while prefix is not prefix of ARR[k]
      remove last character from prefix
      if prefix is empty return ""
  return prefix
```

```

```

Pseudocode (KMP LPS computation and search):
```
COMPUTE-LPS(P)
  lps[1] = 0
  len = 0
  i = 2
  while i <= length[P]
    if P[i] == P[len+1]
      len = len + 1
      lps[i] = len
      i = i + 1
    else if len != 0
      len = lps[len]
    else
      lps[i] = 0
      i = i + 1
  return lps

KMP-SEARCH(T, P)
  lps = COMPUTE-LPS(P)
  i = 1; j = 1
  while i <= length[T]
    if T[i] == P[j]
      i = i + 1; j = j + 1
      if j > length[P] return i - length[P]
    else if j != 1
      j = lps[j-1] + 1
    else
      i = i + 1
  return -1
```
What is the LPS array produced by COMPUTE-LPS('ababaca')? (format like [0,0,1,2,3,0,1])

```
      return S[i]
  return NULL
```


Pseudocode:
```
IS-ANAGRAM(S1, S2)
  if length[S1] != length[S2] return FALSE
  freq = map char->0
  for i = 1 to length[S1]
    freq[S1[i]] = freq[S1[i]] + 1
    freq[S2[i]] = freq[S2[i]] - 1
  for each value v in freq
    if v != 0 return FALSE
  return TRUE
```

```
GROUP-ANAGRAMS(ARR)

  return map.values()
```

Pseudocode (simple numeric check):
```
IS-VALID-NUMBER(S)
  i = 1
  if S[i] == '+' or S[i] == '-' then i = i + 1
  hasDigits = FALSE
  hasDot = FALSE
  while i <= length[S]
    if '0' <= S[i] <= '9' then hasDigits = TRUE
    else if S[i] == '.' and not hasDot then hasDot = TRUE
    else return FALSE
    i = i + 1
  return hasDigits
```
Is '123.45' a valid number according to IS-VALID-NUMBER?

Pseudocode:
```
STRING-TO-INT(S)
  sign = 1; i = 1; if S[1]=='-' then sign=-1; i++
  value = 0
  for ; i <= length[S]; i++
    value = value * 10 + (ord(S[i]) - ord('0'))
  return sign * value
```
What is STRING-TO-INT('1234')?

Pseudocode:
```
INT-TO-STRING(n)
  if n == 0 return "0"
  sign = ''
  if n < 0 then sign = '-' ; n = -n
  digits = empty list
  while n > 0
    append char('0' + n % 10) to digits
    n = n / 10
  reverse digits
  return sign + join(digits)
```
What is INT-TO-STRING(-456)?

Pseudocode (add strings):
```
ADD-STR(A,B)
  i = length[A]; j = length[B]; carry = 0; res = ""
  while i>0 or j>0 or carry>0
    da = (i>0) ? ord(A[i])-ord('0') : 0
    db = (j>0) ? ord(B[j])-ord('0') : 0
    sum = da + db + carry
    append char(ord('0') + (sum % 10)) to front of res
    carry = sum / 10
    i-- ; j--
  return res
```
What is ADD-STR('123','987')?

Pseudocode (grade-school multiply):
```
MULTIPLY-STR(A,B)
  if A=='0' or B=='0' return '0'
  m = length[A]; n = length[B]
  prod = array of zeros of size m+n
  for i = m downto 1
    for j = n downto 1
      prod[i+j] += (A[i]-'0')*(B[j]-'0')
  carry normalize prod to digits
  return string from prod skipping leading zeros
```
What is MULTIPLY-STR('12','11')?

```

```

Pseudocode:
```
DEC-TO-BIN(n)
  if n == 0 return '0'

    append char('0' + (n % 2)) to front of bits
    n = n / 2
  return join(bits)
```

```
VALID-EMAIL(S)
  posAt = find index of '@' in S
  if posAt <= 1 return FALSE
  posDot = find last index of '.' in S
  if posDot < posAt + 2 then return FALSE
  if posDot == length[S] then return FALSE
  return TRUE
```

Pseudocode:
```
INT-TO-STRING(n)
  if n == 0 return "0"
  sign = ""
  if n < 0 then sign = "-"; n = -n
  digits = empty list
  while n > 0
    append char('0' + (n % 10)) to digits
    n = n / 10
  reverse digits
  return sign + join(digits)
```
What is INT-TO-STRING(0)?

Pseudocode:
```
ADD-STR(A,B)
  i = length[A]; j = length[B]
  carry = 0
  res = empty string
  while i > 0 or j > 0 or carry > 0
    da = (i>0) ? (ord(A[i]) - ord('0')) : 0
    db = (j>0) ? (ord(B[j]) - ord('0')) : 0
    sum = da + db + carry
    prepend char(ord('0') + (sum % 10)) to res
    carry = sum / 10
    i = i - 1; j = j - 1
  return res
```
What is ADD-STR('999','1')?

Pseudocode:
```
MULTIPLY-STR(A,B)
  if A == "0" or B == "0" return "0"
  m = length[A]; n = length[B]
  prod = array of zeros of size m+n
  for i = m downto 1
    for j = n downto 1
      prod[i+j-1] += (ord(A[i]) - ord('0')) * (ord(B[j]) - ord('0'))
  // normalize carries
  for k = m+n-1 downto 1
    carry = prod[k] / 10
    prod[k] = prod[k] % 10
    prod[k-1] += carry
  convert prod to string skipping leading zeros
```
What is MULTIPLY-STR('12','12')?

Pseudocode (naive pattern matching):
```
NAIVE-SEARCH(T,P)
  n = length[T]; m = length[P]
  for i = 1 to n - m + 1
    match = TRUE
    for j = 1 to m
      if T[i+j-1] != P[j]
        match = FALSE; break
    if match return i
  return -1
```

Pseudocode (Rabin-Karp with simple rolling hash):
```
RK-SEARCH(T,P)
  // compute hash of P and first window of T
  // roll hash over T and compare; on hash match verify
  return index of first match or -1
```
Given T = 'ababa' and P = 'aba', what index does RK-SEARCH return (1-based)?

Pseudocode (Z-algorithm):
```
COMPUTE-Z(S)
  Z[1] = 0
  l = r = 0
  for i = 2 to length[S]
    if i <= r then Z[i] = min(r-i+1, Z[i-l+1])
    while i+Z[i]-1 < length[S] and S[Z[i]+1] == S[i+Z[i]]
      Z[i] = Z[i] + 1
    if i+Z[i]-1 > r then l = i; r = i+Z[i]-1
  return Z
```

Pseudocode (expand-around-centers):
```
COUNT-PAL-SUBS(S)
  count = 0
  for center = 1 to length[S]
    // odd length
    l = r = center
    while l >= 1 and r <= length[S] and S[l] == S[r]
      count++ ; l-- ; r++
    // even length
    l = center; r = center+1
    while l >= 1 and r <= length[S] and S[l] == S[r]
      count++ ; l-- ; r++
  return count
```
What is COUNT-PAL-SUBS('aaa')?

Pseudocode (expand centers to find longest):
```
LONGEST-PAL-SUBSTR(S)
  best = ""
  for i = 1 to length[S]
    // expand odd
    l=r=i
    while l>=1 and r<=length[S] and S[l]==S[r]
      if r-l+1 > length[best] then best = S[l..r]
      l-- ; r++
    // expand even
    l=i; r=i+1
    while l>=1 and r<=length[S] and S[l]==S[r]
      if r-l+1 > length[best] then best = S[l..r]
      l-- ; r++
  return best
```
What is LONGEST-PAL-SUBSTR('cbbd')?

Pseudocode (string compression):
```
COMPRESS(S)
  res = ""
  count = 1
  for i = 2 to length[S]
    if S[i] == S[i-1] then count = count + 1
    else
      res = res + S[i-1] + toString(count)
      count = 1
  res = res + S[length[S]] + toString(count)
  return res
```
What is COMPRESS('aabccccc')?

Pseudocode (decompress run-length):
```
DECOMPRESS(S)
  res = ""
  i = 1
  while i <= length[S]
    ch = S[i]; i = i + 1
    num = 0
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] is digit
      num = num * 10 + (S[i]-'0'); i = i + 1
    append ch repeated num times to res
  return res
```

Pseudocode (Caesar cipher encrypt shift=3):
```
CAESAR-ENC(S)
  for i = 1 to length[S]
    if 'a' <= S[i] <= 'z' then S[i] = char('a' + (ord(S[i]) - ord('a') + 3) % 26)
    else if 'A' <= S[i] <= 'Z' then similar shift
  return S
```


Pseudocode (Vigenère encrypt high-level):
```
VIGENERE-ENC(plain, key)
  for i = 1 to length[plain]
    shift = ord(key[(i-1) mod length[key]+1]) - ord('A')
    cipher[i] = shift plain[i] by shift
  return cipher
```
Standard example: VIGENERE-ENC('ATTACKATDAWN','LEMON') results in which ciphertext?

Pseudocode (Vigenère decrypt):
```
VIGENERE-DEC(cipher, key)
  // reverse operation of VIGENERE-ENC
```

```
CAN-BUILD(target, source)
  freq = map char->0
  for i = 1 to length[source]

  for j = 1 to length[target]
    if freq[target[j]] == 0 return FALSE
    freq[target[j]]--
  return TRUE
```

Pseudocode:
```
REVERSE-EACH-WORD(S)
  // reverse each word but keep words order
  i = 1
  result = ""
  while i <= length[S]
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] == ' ' do i = i + 1
    start = i
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] != ' ' do i = i + 1
    end = i - 1
    // append reversed word S[start..end]
    for j = end downto start
      result = result + S[j]
    if i <= length[S] then result = result + ' '
  return result
```

```
    if length[w] > length[maxW] then maxW = w
  return (minW, maxW)
```

```
  return join(res,' ')
```

Pseudocode:
```
ATOI(S)
  i = 1; sign = 1
  if S[i] == '-' then sign = -1; i = i + 1
  else if S[i] == '+' then i = i + 1
  val = 0
  while i <= length[S]
    val = val * 10 + (ord(S[i]) - ord('0'))
    i = i + 1
  return sign * val
```
What is ATOI('-42')?

Pseudocode:
```
ITOA(n)
  if n == 0 return "0"
  sign = ''
  if n < 0 then sign = '-'; n = -n
  digits = empty list
  while n > 0
    append char('0' + (n % 10)) to digits
    n = n / 10
  reverse digits
  return sign + join(digits)
```
What is ITOA(-120)?

```
  j = length[S]; while j >= 1 and S[j] == ' ' do j = j - 1
  if i > j return ""
  return S[i..j]
```

```
STRNCMP(S1,S2,n)
  for i = 1 to n
    if S1[i] < S2[i] return -1
    if S1[i] > S2[i] return 1
    if S1[i] == '\0' and S2[i] == '\0' return 0
  return 0
```

Pseudocode (strcpy: copy including terminating null):
```
STRCPY(dest, src)
  i = 1
  while src[i] != '\0'
    dest[i] = src[i]
    i = i + 1
  dest[i] = '\0'
  return dest
```

Pseudocode (strncpy: copy at most n chars, no guarantee of null if n<=len):
```
STRNCPY(dest, src, n)
  i = 1
  while i <= n and src[i] != '\0'
    dest[i] = src[i]
    i = i + 1
  while i <= n
    dest[i] = '\0'
    i = i + 1
  return dest
```

Pseudocode (strcmp: lexicographic compare):
```
STRCMP(S1,S2)
  i = 1
  while S1[i] != '\0' and S2[i] != '\0'
    if S1[i] != S2[i] return (S1[i] < S2[i]) ? -1 : 1
    i = i + 1
  if S1[i] == S2[i] return 0
  return (S1[i] == '\0') ? -1 : 1
```
What does STRCMP('apple','apricot') return (as -1/0/1)?

Pseudocode (strrev: reverse in-place):
```
STRREV(S)
  i = 1; j = length[S]
  while i < j
    swap S[i], S[j]
    i = i + 1; j = j - 1
  return S
```

Pseudocode (strncat: append at most n chars from src):
```
STRNCAT(dest, src, n)
  i = length[dest]
  j = 1
  while j <= n and src[j] != '\0'
    dest[i+1] = src[j]
    i = i + 1; j = j + 1
  dest[i+1] = '\0'
  return dest
```

```
RECURSIVE-STRSTR(T,P)

  if length[T] < length[P] return -1
  if T[1..length[P]] == P return 1
  sub = RECURSIVE-STRSTR(T[2..end], P)
  return (sub == -1) ? -1 : sub + 1
```
